content.full_text:
CITY OF RANCHO MIRAGE

July 31, 1991


Fair Political Practices Commission
Executive Division
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Opinion Letter Request

Gentlemen:

I am requesting of the Fair Political Practices Commission an
opinion regarding the potential conflicts and/or incompatibility of
offices that may exist within the following circumstances.

Rancho Mirage has a district form of government. Each
councilmember is elected to office by those registered voters
within the respective district. The City Council sits as the
Redevelopment Agency Directors. A problem has arisen because one
councilmember is also a member of the Board of Directors of a
Homeowners Association existing within his district. The
Homeowners Association CC&R's have jurisdiction over a large area
within this district which includes property zoned for residential
and commercial uses. However, the CC&R's prohibit commercial uses
upon certain of the residential lots. In addition, the Association
requires annual dues from each parcel as well as a fee, based upon
square footage, for any architectural structure erected within the
area governed by the CC&R's.

About four years ago, the City Redevelopment Agency condemned
several of these residential lots for a City Hall and commercial
site. The City Hall and commercial uses are consistent with the
City's General Plan and zoning for the area but inconsistent with
the CC&R's.

Recently, the Association Board of Directors requested of the
Redevelopment Agency the payment of annual dues per residential
lot. In addition, the Association has previously requested a fee
to review architectural design upon these lots. It is possible for
the Association to not only demand these contributions but also
allege that the CC&R's prohibit commercial and public use of the
lots. I have cited to the Association the appropriate Health and

69-825 HIGHWAY 111 / RANCHO MIRAGE, CA 92270 / (619) 324-4511 / FAX (619) 324-8830

Fair Political Practices Commission
Page 2

Safety Code sections that invalidate CC&R's when the land is
acquired by a Redevelopment Agency for Redevelopment purposes.

An apparent conflict now exists between the Redevelopment Agency
and the Association. The Redevelopment Agency has opined that the
property it condemned is not restricted by the CC&R's, the annual
fee and any other fees mentioned within those documents is invalid
as against the property owned by the Redevelopment Agency. The
Association has alleged an opposite position.

As a member of the Association board, the Redevelopment Agency
Director will be required to vote regarding how the Association is
to proceed against the Agency. The demands of the Association
against the Redevelopment Agency will exceed $50,000 for the
architectural review fee alone. I have opined that there is at
least an incompatibility of offices and/or a conflict of interest
for the reasons set forth in section 18702.5(f).

Your thoughts and opinion on these circumstances would be greatly
appreciated. If there are additional facts you require, do not
hesitate to call.

Sincerely,

Jean Ledard Harris
City Attorney

JLH:dh

Aug 1 | 02 PM '91

sections.question: Whether a conflict of interest and/or incompatibility of offices exists when a city councilmember/Redevelopment Agency Director is also a member of the Board of Directors of a Homeowners Association within his district, where the Association is demanding fees and asserting CC&R restrictions against property condemned by the Redevelopment Agency for public use, requiring the director to vote on the Association's position against the Agency.
sections.question_synthetic: Does a councilmember who serves as a Redevelopment Agency Director face a conflict of interest or incompatibility of offices when also serving on the Board of Directors of a Homeowners Association that is in dispute with the Agency over fees and CC&R restrictions on condemned property?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Incoming request letter from City Attorney to FPPC; no response/conclusion section present; references Political Code section 18702.5(f); document is the original request for opinion, not FPPC's response

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether a conflict of interest and/or incompatibility of offices exists when a city councilmember/Redevelopment Agency Director is also a member of the Board of Directors of a Homeowners Association within his district, where the Association is demanding fees and asserting CC&R restrictions against property condemned by the Redevelopment Agency for public use, requiring the director to vote on the Association's position against the Agency.

embedding.summary: City of Rancho Mirage requests an FPPC opinion regarding potential conflicts of interest and incompatibility of offices for a councilmember/Redevelopment Agency Director who also serves on a Homeowners Association board that is disputing the Agency's condemned property with potential claims exceeding $50,000.
